text.title: Gce Instance Templates Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for the gce-instance-templates methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale + concrete remediation hook.

rule.statement: Instance templates are immutable; ANY change MUST produce a new template version (suffixed v2/v3 or by date).
rule.rationale: Editing in place is not supported; treating templates as versioned artefacts makes rollbacks trivial.

rule.statement: Templates MUST assign a dedicated service account with least-privileged scopes — never the default compute SA with full cloud-platform scope.
rule.rationale: Default SA grants broad project-wide access; per-template SA contains blast radius.

rule.statement: Templates MUST enable OS Login + IAP TCP forwarding; SSH keys in instance metadata are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Metadata-key SSH bypasses IAM and audit; OS Login ties access to IAM identities with full logging.

rule.statement: Startup scripts MUST be idempotent and check current state before mutating (no blind `apt install` loops without check).
rule.rationale: Non-idempotent scripts fail re-execution after auto-heal, producing partially configured instances.

rule.statement: Secrets MUST NOT be embedded in instance metadata or startup scripts; use Secret Manager + Workload Identity instead.
rule.rationale: Metadata is readable from inside the VM by any process; embedded secrets leak via local-account compromise.

rule.statement: If the input fails ALL "Applies If" preconditions or matches ANY "Skip If" disqualifier, do not apply this methodology — route the task to the adjacent sibling instead.
rule.rationale: Even flat checklists need an explicit skip leaf in the decision tree; this rule is the universal terminal.
